triggerPath: Selected inner vector object shares color with unselected outer artwork and the operator wants only the selected object filled.
failureMode: Before this slice, the only obvious workflow was changing the shared color layer to Fill, which filled every same-color closed contour.
consequence: The full design could become black/fill instead of only the selected inner artwork.
concreteFix: Add an object-scoped Fill Selection command that isolates selected vector objects onto a fill layer when their color is shared with unselected artwork.

triggerPath: Multiple closed same-color objects are compiled on a Fill layer.
failureMode: Not a failure: same-layer nested and overlapping objects interact as a compound fill set.
consequence: Correct layer-level CAM behavior can surprise users unless object-scoped commands exist.
concreteFix: Keep the behavior tested; improve UX with object-scoped fill, break-apart, and path selection.

triggerPath: Imported SVG contains outer and inner contours inside one SceneObject, but the operator wants only one contour filled.
failureMode: Layer assignment operates at whole-object granularity. The expanded Break Apart action now fixes imported SVGs with multiple paths or multiple contours inside one path, but it is still an explode workflow rather than live node/subpath editing.
consequence: Fill Selection plus Break Apart fixes common separate-path and same-path multi-contour import cases, but true path/node editing is still missing.
concreteFix: Keep contour-level Break Apart; add true on-canvas subpath/node editing before claiming full drawing-tool parity.

triggerPath: Selected imported SVG has multiple ColoredPath entries or one ColoredPath with multiple polylines, and the operator needs one inner/outer contour as its own selectable object.
failureMode: Before these slices, the imported SVG could only be selected and layer-assigned as one object.
consequence: Users could not isolate a nested imported SVG contour before using Fill Selection.
concreteFix: Expand Break Apart to replace a selected imported SVG with one imported-svg object per contour/polyline, preserving transform, source lineage, color, bounds, undo, and selection.

triggerPath: Selected vector artwork is assigned to an output Fill layer but contains one or more open contours.
failureMode: Before this slice, open contours were silently skipped by fill hatching without immediate design-time feedback.
consequence: Users could think Fill is broken or filling the wrong region, especially after imported or traced artwork.
concreteFix: Show a status bar warning that selected open contours on output Fill layers will not fill, using the shared isClosedEnough predicate.

triggerPath: Selected vector artwork is assigned to an output Fill layer and contains open contours.
failureMode: The first warning slice reported the count in the status bar but did not identify the failing contour visually.
consequence: Users still had to manually hunt through imported or traced artwork to find the open contour that would not fill.
concreteFix: Render selected open Fill contours with a dashed orange warning stroke using the shared selectedOpenFillContours diagnostic helper.

triggerPath: Selected vector artwork is assigned to an output Fill layer, contains an open contour, and the contour's start/end gap is within 0.5 mm.
failureMode: The status warning and canvas highlight could identify the open Fill contour, but LaserForge had no in-app repair command for near-closed contours.
consequence: Users had to leave LaserForge or manually redraw otherwise fillable artwork.
concreteFix: Add Close Open Fill Contours, which marks only selected, unlocked, output-Fill contours within the safe endpoint tolerance as closed.

triggerPath: Selected vector artwork is assigned to an output Fill layer, contains an open contour, and the contour's endpoint gap is wider than the safe 0.5 mm quick-close threshold.
failureMode: Before this slice, LaserForge could warn and highlight the open contour but could not offer a reviewed in-app repair for wider intentional endpoint gaps.
consequence: Users had to leave LaserForge or redraw otherwise recoverable Fill geometry.
concreteFix: Add Close Fill Contours With Tolerance..., preview open/safe/reviewed/remaining counts, and apply only selected unlocked output-Fill contours within the reviewed tolerance as one undoable mutation.

triggerPath: Operator needs union, difference, intersection, weld, or cut-shape editing to create the desired filled region.
failureMode: No geometry-kernel-backed boolean editing workflow is available in the app.
consequence: LaserForge remains behind LightBurn-class design editing for complex filled shapes.
concreteFix: Stage pure-core boolean tools with fixtures and visual golden checks before UI.

triggerPath: Operator needs to repair a contour, edit trace nodes, or convert a primitive/text object into editable path geometry.
failureMode: A first Node tool can select, keyboard-nudge, and mouse-drag vector nodes, but LaserForge still lacks segment/subpath selection, insert/delete node operations, Bezier handle editing, Move Ends Together preview, and Convert to Path for text/primitive objects.
consequence: Open shapes and imperfect imported/trace geometry are easier to inspect and adjust, but LaserForge remains behind LightBurn-class path editing.
concreteFix: Continue the Node tool with segment selection, insert/delete operations, curve handles, and Convert to Path once path edits are fully persisted.
